scope.full_suite_note: Repository-wide test failures (psutil/filelock-dependent tests in test_atomic_io etc.) are pre-existing environment issues unrelated to this milestone — same failure count before and after these changes.

gap_summaries: [post-impl] M1: _find_existing_test docstring claimed 'file-read errors propagate naturally as OSError', but path.read_text(encoding='utf-8') can also raise UnicodeDecodeError (subclass of ValueError, NOT OSError) on a non-UTF-8 file. Fixed: docstring now lists both OSError and UnicodeDecodeError as propagable.

[post-impl] M2: PlanFeatureTestsDryRunTests.test_dry_run_does_not_write_file constructed Path(entry.created_test_path) without first asserting it is not None — inconsistent with the parallel test_creates_skeleton_when_no_existing_test, which does assertIsNotNone before the Path() call. Fixed: added assertIsNotNone(entry.created_test_path) for symmetry and to make the None-narrowing visible to static type checkers.

[production] P1: Module declared logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) but emitted zero log records — an automation utility that walks REQs and writes files should provide actionable observability so the M06b orchestrator (and operators) can see which REQs were found, created, or skipped without re-deriving from the returned plan. Added logger.info() emissions at all three TestPlanEntry branch points (found/created/skipped) carrying req_id and resolved path; also factored str(target.resolve()) into target_str so the path is computed exactly once per non-found verdict instead of twice.

production_readiness.security: ok — REQ id strictly validated via REQ-\d+ fullmatch; tests_dir glob restricted to test_compliance_*.py; write goes through write_atomic_text (M05 atomic + per-path-lock semantics); no secrets, no SQL, no shell.
production_readiness.error_resilience: ok — OSError/UnicodeDecodeError propagate by design; atomic_io handles Windows ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION retries upstream; no swallowed exceptions.
production_readiness.testing: ok — no time.sleep, no network, no subprocess; tests use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory; assertions verify exact values (golden-string byte-equality + path equality).
production_readiness.code_quality: ok — zero TODO/FIXME/HACK markers; follows CLAUDE.md conventions (from __future__ import annotations, @dataclass(kw_only=True, frozen=True), PEP 604 unions, ruff clean, ruff format clean).
